name: building-logseq-plugins
description: Expert guidance for building Logseq plugins compatible with the new DB architecture. Auto-invokes when users want to create Logseq plugins, work with the Logseq Plugin API, extend Logseq functionality, or need help with plugin development for DB-based graphs. Covers plugin structure, API usage, and DB-specific considerations.
capabilities: Guide plugin structure and file organization
Explain logseq.Editor, logseq.DB, and logseq.App namespaces
Provide Datalog query examples for DB version
Show slash command and block context menu registration
Explain DB vs MD version compatibility patterns
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only guidance text and code examples for Logseq plugin development. All 60 static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code fences, URLs are documentation links, and content_hash fields are metadata. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file operations exist.
valueStatement: Building Logseq plugins requires understanding the Plugin API, DB architecture, and compatibility patterns. This skill provides expert guidance on plugin structure, API namespaces, Datalog queries, and DB-version specific considerations.
userTitle: Build Logseq Plugins with DB Support
limitations: Does not write plugin code or create files directly
Does not interact with Logseq application or execute plugins
Does not install or configure development environments
Limited to guidance; actual development requires separate setup

useCases.title: Extend Logseq Functionality

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic Plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I create a new Logseq plugin? Show me the basic file structure and package.json manifest.

promptTemplates.title: Query Graph Data
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I write a Datalog query to find all blocks with a specific tag in the DB version?

promptTemplates.title: Register Slash Commands
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I register a slash command and a block context menu item in my Logseq plugin?

promptTemplates.title: Handle Properties
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the differences in handling block properties between MD and DB versions of Logseq?

bestPractices: Use feature detection to check if the graph is DB-based before executing version-specific code
Test plugins with both DB and MD graphs when supporting both versions
Follow the plugin structure convention with package.json, index.html, and README.md
antiPatterns: Hardcoding page names instead of using configurable settings
Assuming MD-style queries work in DB version without modification
Not handling the unified node model where pages and blocks are similar entities

faq.question: What is the difference between MD and DB versions of Logseq?
faq.answer: MD version uses file-based storage while DB version uses a Datascript database. DB version offers structured properties, typed values, and entity-based queries.

faq.question: What tools do I need to develop Logseq plugins?
faq.answer: Node.js and npm for package management, a code editor, and Logseq with developer mode enabled for testing.

faq.question: How do I load my plugin for testing?
faq.answer: Enable developer mode in Logseq settings, then go to Plugins and select Load unpacked plugin to choose your folder.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using Logseq plugins?
faq.answer: Plugins run in sandboxed iframes with limited API access. Review plugin permissions before installing third-party plugins.

faq.question: Why is my MD query not working in DB version?
faq.answer: DB version uses entity-based queries with :block/title instead of :block/content. Rewrite queries using Datalog syntax for the DB schema.

faq.question: How do I publish my plugin to the Logseq marketplace?
faq.answer: Build your plugin with npm run build, create a GitHub release, and submit your plugin to the Logseq Plugin Marketplace repository.
